dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7074 IH: Keeping Public Lands Out of Adversarial Hands Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Newhouse (for himself, Mr. Hurd of Colorado, Mr. Rose, Mrs. Hinson, and Mr. Ezell) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services, and in addition to the Committees on Energy and Commerce, and Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend the Defense Production Act of 1950 to include the Secretary of the Interior on the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Keeping Public Lands Out of Adversarial Hands Act.

section.header: Inclusion of the Secretary of the Interior on the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States and consideration of certain land or resource transactions

subsection.text: Section 721(k) of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (50 U.S.C. 4565(k)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.header: Inclusion of the Secretary of the Interior
paragraph.text: The Secretary of the Interior shall be a member of the Committee with respect to a covered transaction that involves land or resources adjacent to land or sites owned or managed by—

subparagraph.text: the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management;
the United States Fish and Wildlife Service.

subsection.header: Consideration of certain land or resource transactions
subsection.text: Section 721(b)(1) of the Defense Production Act of 1950 (50 U.S.C. 4565(b)(1)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.header: Consideration of certain land or resource transactions

clause.text: After receiving notification from the Secretary of the Interior of a reportable land or resource transaction, the Committee shall determine—

subclause.text: whether the transaction is a covered transaction; and

if the Committee determines that the transaction is a covered transaction, whether the Committee should initiate a review pursuant to subparagraph (D), or take another action authorized under this section, with respect to the reportable land or resource transaction.

clause.text: In this subparagraph, the term reportable land or resource transaction means a transaction—

subclause.text: that the Secretary of the Interior has reason to believe is a covered transaction; and

that involves the acquisition of an interest in land or resource by a foreign person of the People’s Republic of China, the Democratic People’s Republic of Korea, the Russian Federation, or the Islamic Republic of Iran.

clause.text: The requirements under this subparagraph shall terminate, with respect to a foreign person of the respective foreign country, on the date that the People’s Republic of China, the Democratic People’s Republic of Korea, the Russian Federation, or the Islamic Republic of Iran, as the case may be, is removed from the list of foreign adversaries in section 791.4 of title 15, Code of Federal Regulations.
